steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim states that Winnemucca “creates suspense by emphasizing her physical response to an event.” The correct quotation must show repeated or heightened attention to a bodily reaction — not just describe an event.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘That same fall, after my grandfather came home...’: Reports a sequence of events involving her grandfather and father. No physical response is described. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Oh, how my heart jumped when I heard a noise close by...Oh, how my heart beat!’: Twice calls attention to a bodily reaction (heart jumping, heart beating), explicitly asking whether an outside person could hear it — this is emphasis on physical response used to build suspense. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Oh, what a pretty dress my sister got!...’: Describes hiding and observing a dress. No physical response, no suspense-building. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Late in that fall, there came news that my grandfather was on his way home...’: Reports news and a gathering of people. No physical response is described. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
